title: Stream xlsx return a corrupted file [Excel js 4.2.0]
author: Djcharles26

comments.author: m-janos
comments.body: I got similar issue. Rolling back the version of Node.js (from 15.6.0) to some older helped me. The exact cause is still unknown.

comments.author: Djcharles26

comments.author: trypton
comments.body: Same here with nodejs 15.7, downgrading to nodejs 14 helped. The problem is that all files (styles, theme, workbook) in the xlsx container have 0 bytes size (except `sheet1.xml`).

comments.author: alexbjorlig
comments.body: Did anyone find a work-around to avoid downgrading node.js?

comments.author: ybonnefond
jpmnteiro
comments.body:
Running into a similar issue. Unfortunately deleting the lock file is not an option (though it does solve the issue).

What I found, however, is that the problem seems to be related to archiver (or one of its dependencies).

If I decompress the xlsx and re-compress it into another xlsx (e.g using `zip`), the resulting xlsx is valid, despite not having changed any of the files.

Another interesting tidbit:

```
$ unzip 20230125T150005Z_excel.xlsx -d bad/
Archive:  20230125T150005Z_excel.xlsx
  inflating: bad/xl/theme/theme1.xml   bad CRC df309b76  (should be 00000000)
  inflating: bad/_rels/.rels          bad CRC da499ff2  (should be 00000000)
  inflating: bad/xl/worksheets/sheet1.xml  
  inflating: bad/[Content_Types].xml   bad CRC c331fee1  (should be 00000000)
  inflating: bad/docProps/app.xml     bad CRC 6a6f329f  (should be 00000000)
  inflating: bad/docProps/core.xml    bad CRC 5d5d0095  (should be 00000000)
  inflating: bad/xl/styles.xml        bad CRC cfa762fa  (should be 00000000)
  inflating: bad/xl/_rels/workbook.xml.rels   bad CRC 3a6ee34e  (should be 00000000)
  inflating: bad/xl/workbook.xml      bad CRC e7ee1e6a  (should be 00000000)
```
Is a good indication that something's wrong with the zip structure. The CRCs are correct:

```
$ crc32 ./bad/docProps/core.xml 
5d5d0095
```

----

Edit:

My suspicion lies heavily on archiver.

I wrote a workaround that decompresses the xlsx using `unzipper` and re-compresses with `archiver` and the xlsx still comes out corrupt.

However, if I use `zip` instead (via `child_process.execSync`), the resulting xlsx is valid.

Really a head scratcher.

comments.author: kibertoad
comments.body:
This is due to a bug in `unzipper`, which has known compatibility issues with newer Node versions, e. g. see https://github.com/ZJONSSON/node-unzipper/issues/271

We've replaced it with `extract-zip` after hitting in prod, but generally I'd say that https://www.npmjs.com/package/yauzl-promise is the best unzipping solution right now

comments.author: aphougat
